"But," I went on, "for secreting pearls, the ideal mollusk is the pearl oyster Meleagrina margaritifera, that valuable shellfish. Pearls result simply from mother-of-pearl solidifying into a globular shape. Either they stick to the oyster's shell, or they become embedded in the creature's folds. On the valves a pearl sticks fast; on the flesh it lies loose. But its nucleus is always some small, hard object, say a sterile egg or a grain of sand, around which the mother-of-pearl is deposited in thin, concentric layers over several years in succession."

The polar island is small and volcanic. It measures "not more than eight or ten miles in circumference" (ACH-055-025), a compact cone rising alone from the sea, and it vents fire from a single vent: "there was only one crater to the volcano" (ACH-055-015), out of which columns of flame and smoke pour continuously.

New Aberfoyle is the immense subterranean coalfield discovered beneath the exhausted pits of old [[Aberfoyle]] in *The Underground City*. When Simon Ford's long-suspected new vein is finally reached, the cry goes up that "the road to New Aberfoyle is open!" (UC-007-028) It is a natural cavern-world of galleries, vaults, and lakes far surpassing the mine it replaces.

Tomsk sits on the severed telegraph line running east across Siberia. The wire "extending from Ekaterenburg to Nikolaevsk... had been cut, first beyond Tomsk, and then between Tomsk and Kolyvan," (MS-003-006) fixing the city as the frontier between the connected and the isolated halves of the empire.
